name: Western Union
description_short: At 1301 Long Beach Blvd, Long Beach, CA 90813, Western Union provides check cashing and money transfer services. Call +1 562-599-1550.
description_long:
Western Union is an established financial services company operating a massive network of agent locations across the United States and internationally. The company enables consumers to send money domestically and internationally, with recipients able to pick up cash at physical locations or receive funds directly to cards. Beyond money transfers, Western Union provides a range of complementary financial services including bill payment, money orders, mobile top-ups, and inmate payment services.

The company operates through a hybrid model combining online initiation with in-store payment and pickup options. Customers can start transfers online 24/7 through WU.com, then complete payment at agent locations (which include major retailers like Walmart, Safeway, City Market, and Kroger) during their business hours. The platform offers tracking functionality, a Western Union Rewards program, and the ability to refer friends. They also provide Western Union Prepaid card services and delivery method updates for greater flexibility.

Western Union's primary distinction is its massive physical footprint—the website references 150+ locations in a single Colorado search result, indicating tens of thousands of locations nationwide. This extensive agent network is their competitive advantage for consumers who prefer in-person transactions or need same-day cash access. The company's multi-language support (English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, Vietnamese) reflects their focus on immigrant and international remittance communities.

However, Western Union's money transfer services typically carry higher fees compared to digital-only competitors, though this is offset by accessibility and speed. The company's strength lies in physical convenience and established trust rather than cost leadership. Their service is best suited for consumers prioritizing immediate cash access and physical transaction options over minimizing transfer fees.
address: 1301 Long Beach Blvd, Long Beach, CA 90813

company_info.headquarters: 1301 Long Beach Blvd, Long Beach, CA 90813

pros: Massive agent network with 150+ locations shown in single state search, including major retailers (Walmart, Safeway, City Market, Kroger)
Hybrid 24/7 online initiation combined with same-day in-store payment and pickup options
Multi-language support in 6 languages (English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, Vietnamese)
Track transfer feature provides real-time monitoring of money sent
Western Union Rewards program offers incentives for repeat customers
Accepts multiple payment methods and offers prepaid card options
Additional services including bill payment, mobile top-ups, and money orders reduce need for multiple providers
cons: Transfer fees are higher than digital-only money transfer competitors like Wise or PayPal
Recipient pickup limited to agent location hours, which may not be 24/7 despite online availability
Website experienced service disruptions (Orange Wallet temporarily unavailable during research)
Some locations limited to money order services only (e.g., Circle K #0682 in Leadville)
Geolocation required for accurate location discovery, creating friction for users
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked consumers who prefer cash transactions and don't have access to digital banking
International remittance senders who need established, trusted services with broad global coverage
Consumers prioritizing same-day cash availability over lowest transfer fees
Immigrant communities sending money to family abroad and needing multi-language support
diagnosis: Western Union is best for consumers who value physical accessibility and speed over competitive pricing—those without bank accounts, immigrants sending remittances, and anyone needing same-day cash access. The main caveat is that their fees significantly exceed digital alternatives like Wise or Remitly, making them more expensive for price-sensitive customers with internet access and bank accounts.
services: International and domestic money transfers with online initiation
